events.note: The Council and the mayor must adopt the fiscal 2027 budget. The final deal reveals actual funding levels for every policy priority.

events.note: The current schools governance law sunsets. Continuing or changing it requires action in Albany.

Liberty Weekend, 40 years on
events.note: In July 1986 the Statue of Liberty's centennial and rededication drew the largest tall-ship parade in modern history, with Reagan presiding.

events.title: America's 250th birthday
events.note: New York Harbor hosts the largest fleet of tall ships and naval vessels ever assembled, echoing the 1976 bicentennial and 1986 Liberty Weekend. Coincides with the 50th Macy's Fourth of July fireworks.

events.title: The Declaration is read to Washington's troops — 250th anniversary
events.note: The Declaration of Independence was read aloud to Continental Army troops assembled on the Common on July 9, 1776, greeted by three cheers.

events.title: The statue of King George III is toppled — 250th anniversary
events.note: After the Declaration was read, a crowd rushed to Bowling Green and pulled down the gilded lead equestrian statue of King George III.

events.note: Fifty years since the convention at Madison Square Garden nominated Carter, in the depths of the city's fiscal crisis.

events.title: The Battle of Long Island — 250th anniversary
events.note: The Battle of Long Island on August 27, 1776, was the largest battle of the Revolution and a British victory that gave them New York for the rest of the war.

events.title: Local Law 97 extended filing deadline
events.note: The final deadline for buildings that paid the $60 extension fee in June.

events.title: One year until Rikers must close
events.note: The statutory deadline to close Rikers Island is Aug. 31, 2027. This marks exactly one year out, and is a fixed point for advocacy and coverage.

events.title: United States Open begins
events.note: Runs through Sept. 13. Major tourism and transit impact for Queens.

events.title: Luigi Mangione's state murder trial begins
events.note: The state trial for the killing of UnitedHealthcare chief executive Brian Thompson in Midtown. Originally set for June 8 and moved to September; the federal trial is pushed to January 2027.

events.title: First day of school
events.note: The 2026-27 school year begins the day after Labor Day. Roughly a million children return to class.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: MWBE_7-858-0662A - HYBRID CLOUD SECURITY ARCHITECT
9/11 attacks destroy the World Trade Center — 25th anniversary
events.note: Al-Qaeda hijackers crashed two jetliners into the twin towers of the World Trade Center, collapsing both and killing 2,753 people in New York City. It was the deadliest terrorist attack in U.S. history.

events.title: The British land at Kip's Bay — 250th anniversary
events.note: British warships and troops landed at Kip's Bay on Manhattan's East River shore on September 15, 1776, scattering American militia.

events.title: Committee on Education
events.note: Jointly with the Committee on Oversight and Investigations · Council Chambers - City Hall
why: subject matter of broad public interest

events.title: Committee on Oversight and Investigations
events.note: Jointly with the Committee on Education. · Council Chambers - City Hall
why: subject matter of broad public interest

events.title: The Battle of Harlem Heights — 250th anniversary
events.note: On September 16, 1776, Washington won his first battlefield victory of the war at Harlem Heights in upper Manhattan.

events.title: Mayor's Management Report due
events.note: The charter-mandated scorecard for every agency: trash, crime, service delivery. The closest thing the city has to a report card.

events.title: The New-York Daily Times debuts — 175th anniversary
events.note: Henry Raymond and George Jones published the first issue of the New-York Daily Times, later The New York Times.

events.title: The Great Fire of 1776 — 250th anniversary
events.note: A fire burned through the night into September 21, 1776, destroying a large swath of British-occupied New York including Trinity Church.

events.title: Nathan Hale is hanged as a spy — 250th anniversary
events.note: The British hanged 21-year-old Continental officer Nathan Hale as a spy in occupied New York on September 22, 1776.

events.title: United Nations General Assembly: leaders' week
events.note: World leaders descend on the city for the high-level debate. Major security, traffic and diplomatic consequences for Midtown.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Transit authority preliminary 2027 budget
events.note: A key indicator of fiscal health and service levels.

events.title: Federal fiscal year ends; shutdown risk
events.note: Federal appropriations expire at midnight. New York City is heavily exposed to disruptions in housing vouchers, Medicaid, transit and disaster aid.

events.title: New rent guidelines take effect
events.note: The board's adjustments begin applying to new and renewal leases.

events.title: Fair housing framework assessment due
events.note: The first-ever needs assessment and five-year affordable housing production targets for all 59 community districts.

events.title: City of Yes: first affordable housing district list
events.note: City Planning publishes the 12 community districts with the lowest affordable housing production.

events.title: Federal fiscal year 2027 begins
events.note: Watch for continuing resolutions and shutdown risk affecting city funding.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Voter registration deadline
events.note: The last day to register for the Nov. 3 general election.

events.title: Committee on Environmental Protection and Waterfronts
Stated meeting of the City Council
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Early voting begins
events.note: A nine-day early voting period for the general election, running through Nov. 1.

events.title: The Battle of White Plains — 250th anniversary
events.note: British and Hessian forces under Howe defeated Washington at White Plains in Westchester on October 28, 1776.

events.title: New York City Marathon: 50 years of the five-borough course
events.note: The marathon first ran through all five boroughs in 1976 for the bicentennial, making 2026 the 50th running of the modern course.

events.title: General election
events.note: Governor (Hochul vs. likely Blakeman), Congress and state legislative races. No mayoral race this year. Hochul-Mamdani coordination is critical for Democratic turnout.

events.title: The Schaghen letter records the 'purchase' of Manhattan — 400th anniversary
events.note: A letter by Dutch official Pieter Schaghen, dated Nov. 5, 1626, reported that colonists had 'purchased' the island of Manhattan from its Native inhabitants for goods worth 60 guilders. It is the only contemporary document of the transaction.

events.title: DC 37's contract expires
events.note: The largest municipal union, roughly 90,000 workers. The expiring 2021-2026 agreement sets the pattern for the teachers' union and everyone else. The first major labor test of this administration.

events.title: Bloomberg elected mayor after 9/11 — 25th anniversary
events.note: Media entrepreneur Michael Bloomberg won the mayoralty just weeks after the September 11 attacks, defeating Democrat Mark Green. He took office as the 108th mayor on Jan. 1, 2002.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Nunez monitor report on Rikers
events.note: The second semi-annual report to Judge Swain. It effectively grades the administration's first year of jail management; a poor grade accelerates the receivership timeline.

events.title: The fall of Fort Washington — 250th anniversary
events.note: British and Hessian forces captured Fort Washington, the last American stronghold on Manhattan, on November 16, 1776.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: November financial plan update
events.note: The city updates revenue and spending projections, setting the baseline for the next budget cycle.

events.title: The 100th Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
events.note: A milestone parade; the count reaches 100 in 2026 because the parade was suspended during the Second World War. A year-long celebration campaign surrounds it.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Transit board adopts the 2027 budget
events.note: The final operating budget for the coming year.

events.title: Stated meeting of the City Council
why: stated meeting of the full Council

events.title: Albany post-election positioning
events.note: The lame-duck period for termed-out legislators; watch for late activity on pending bills.

events.title: Studio 54 opens — 50th anniversary
events.note: The Midtown discotheque opened and instantly became the epicenter of celebrity nightlife and disco excess.

events.title: Lever House brings the glass curtain wall to Park Avenue — 75th anniversary
events.note: Gordon Bunshaft's Lever House, for Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, opened at 390 Park Avenue. Its blue-green glass skin was among the first true curtain walls on an American office tower.

events.title: Lindbergh gets a ticker-tape hero's welcome — 100th anniversary
events.note: Millions lined Broadway for a ticker-tape parade honoring Charles Lindbergh after his solo flight across the Atlantic.

events.title: Slavery ends in New York State — 200th anniversary
events.note: An 1817 law took full effect on July 4, 1827, ending legal slavery in New York and freeing thousands of remaining enslaved people.

events.title: The blackout that broke the city's spirit — 50th anniversary
events.note: Lightning strikes knocked out power for some 25 hours, unleashing widespread looting and arson across the crime-plagued, cash-strapped city.

events.title: The Son of Sam is captured — 50th anniversary
events.note: Police arrested David Berkowitz, ending a serial-killing spree that terrorized the city for more than a year.
